text.title: Agentic BPA Workflow
text.summary: State-machine subagent dispatch for the 5-stage cycle, stage-specific prompt patterns, and discover_dfg.py for event-log-based process discovery.

section.title: State Machine Dispatch

rule.statement: Persist a _bpa-state.json file in the feature's .aidocs directory tracking completed stages and artifact paths. Each agent invocation reads this file to determine which stage to run next. Do not re-run completed stages unless explicitly reset.
rule.rationale: Long BPA sessions span multiple days. Without a state file, a resumed session re-runs early stages and overwrites verified artifacts with less-grounded re-analyses.

rule.statement: Stage 2 and 3 agents are evidence-bound: they receive paths to raw evidence files and must cite them. Stage 4 (design) is generative and receives only the verified stage-3 analysis. Never feed raw evidence to the stage-4 agent directly.
rule.rationale: Feeding raw evidence to the design agent allows it to re-interpret the as-is analysis and drift from the verified findings. The verified analysis is the authoritative input for design.

section.reference: Workflow state file for resumable 5-stage pipeline.
Event-log to directly-follows graph via pm4py; validates manually-built BPMN against log reality.

rule.statement: Stage 2 dispatch: "Read evidence files: [paths]. Build the as-is model for '[process-name]'. For every step row, cite the source line/file in the Notes column. If a step is mentioned by only one source, mark it 'single-source — verify'. Do NOT smooth over contradictions; emit a ## Contradictions section."
rule.rationale: The citation and contradiction requirements are the most important constraints in stage 2. Without them, the agent produces a plausible-sounding but unverifiable process map.

rule.statement: Stage 3 dispatch: "Input: process-documentation.md. Produce process-analysis.md. VA/BN/NVA rubric: [pin examples]. Cycle time and error rate: cite source line or write 'Data not available'. End with Top-3 candidates for elimination ranked by NVA-minutes × frequency."
rule.rationale: The rubric pinning, the data-source requirement, and the frequency-weighted ranking are the three constraints that prevent the most common stage-3 failures.

rule.statement: When event logs (Jira exports, Zendesk ticket histories, GitHub commit logs) are available, run discover_dfg.py to produce a directly-follows graph before stage 2. Compare the discovered DFG against the manually-built process map. Discrepancies equal workarounds that were not mentioned in interviews.
rule.rationale: Process mining discovers the actual execution sequence from log data without bias from what stakeholders say should happen. Discrepancies between the DFG and the interview-based map are the highest-value finding in any BPA engagement.
